title: Parathelypteris hirsutipes
taxonomy: Parathelypteris hirsutipes is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a type of deciduous fern native to East Asia. The classification of Parathelypteris hirsutipes includes the kingdom Plantae, the division Pteridophyta, and the class Polypodiopsida. Further classification also includes the order Polypodiales and the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern is closely related to other species in the Parathelypteris genus.
uses: Parathelypteris hirsutipes, also known as Japanese hairoshidake, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and rhizomes are used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves are also used to make a poultice that is applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Parathelypteris hirsutipes for medicinal purposes.

intro: Parathelypteris hirsutipes, also known as Japanese chain fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of P. hirsutipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
